stem: A industrial safety safety system uses 2 parallel sensors for redundancy. The hazard detected requires at least one sensor to function. sensor 1 has a reliability of 79%. sensor 2 has a reliability between 68% and 76% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the hazard detected?

ground_truth.answer: P(hazard detected) ranges from 0.9328 to 0.9496. Point estimate: 0.9412.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 2 sensors fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.21 × 0.32 = 0.0672. P(works) = 0.9328.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.21 × 0.24 = 0.0504. P(works) = 0.9496.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
